title: Red Star Shipping Lines

'''Red Star Shipping Lines''' was a major shipping organization in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]], owning twelve [[Star system/Legends|systems]] recognized as transit hubs.<ref name="HSCS" /> During the time of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], Red Star, along with [[Pulsar Supertanker]], [[Quasar Cargo]], and [[Ororo Transportation/Legends|Ororo Transportation]] were choking commerce by shutting out [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]-based competitors and refusing to share navigational data. The [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], which began its existence as a Republic-chartered organization charged with dispute mediation, helped to curb these abuses of power.<ref name="EGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> They were one of the [[voting sponsor]]s of the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]], investing in the venture in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the formation of the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]].<ref name="HSCS" /> They owned the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] designated [[Transport A-175]], which was hijacked by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] ''[[Far Orbit]]''. The loss of a cargo of [[Power cell/Legends|power cells]] and power system components cost the company about 35 million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="HS1037">{{WEGCite|book=The Far Orbit Project|story=The Hijacking of Shipment 1037}}</ref>
